messages.bodyPreview:
Hi again Ben,

Thanks for meeting with me.

Please see proposed mutual separation agreement attached, as well as more
information regarding the outplacement support. Regarding a follow up call
tomorrow - would 1:30pm work?

Best,
Ebba

Hi Ebba,

Hope you're doing alright given he circumstances. I'd appreciate it if we
could put in some time later this week (Thursday or Friday) to touch base.
I've been helping organise a few things as I just got back Monday and
people need support too.
With the reorganisation announcements tomorrow and today there's a lot of
worried people so I'm focussing on:
1) rallying the changes in AI, we've been prepping for a while and they're
just panicking when for us, it's just a continuation
2) he...

Hi Ben,

Thanks for the note, and I appreciate you sharing the context.

I also want to acknowledge the care you’re showing for the team during
times of change and many unknowns. At the same time, as Adrian and I shared
in our conversation, we’d strongly encourage you to shift your focus to
yourself at this point. With the reorg communication going out, it’s
important that we use our time to focus on what the path forward will look
like for you. As discussed, the purpose of reconnecting is to...

Hi Ebba,

Thank you for the time yesterday. Our conversation gave me a lot to process.

My partner is taking today off so we can properly evaluate the personal and
financial implications of the current proposal on our life here in Sweden.
I am also currently waiting for Unionen to get back to me on the initial
text of the agreement. They seem to be taking longer than expected to
review the legal ramifications (and I’m no expert on legal text!), so I’ll
be calling them today to check on their...

Hi Ben,

Thanks for sharing your questions. I’ve shared them with Adrian and will
align with him regarding the assessment.

Regarding your question on the nature of the proposed separation, I can
confirm that this is being treated as a leadership decision, with them
considering a mutual separation agreement as the preferred option.

Please take some time over the weekend to consider the settlement
agreement, I fully understand it's a big decision for you and your partner.

Hi Ebba,

Writing to follow up on our last conversation. I haven’t yet received the
written clarifications regarding the nature of the separation and the
assessment methodology used for the new organization. As we discussed,
having these facts in writing is essential for me and my Unionen
representative to review the proposal fairly.

To help us move forward, I need to request that my formal *2025 Talent
Snapshot* be finalised and shared with me. I understand the company-wide
deadline was Jan...

Hi Johan,

Moving this conversation to my personal email thread (where I was
previously communicating with Ebba) to ensure I have persistent access to
our correspondence. Please ensure all future correspondence regarding this
matter is directed to this address (benpurslow@gmail.com).

Thank you for the written clarification. It is helpful to have your
confirmation that this is *"not a retrospective audit of my technical track
record"* but rather a forward-looking leadership decision. This con...

Hi Johan,

Thank you for the conversation yesterday. I appreciate the open
conversation and the direction we're heading. I wanted to capture what we
discussed so we're aligned on next steps.

Agreed next steps from yesterday:

   1. We agreed to explore both the mutual separation agreement and
   potential IC redeployment in parallel, rather than sequentially. Both
   tracks remain open.
   2. I will work on identifying areas where my skills and experience align
   with business needs in an I...

Hi Ben,

Thanks for the summary. The steps reflect my view well, and I have nothing
to add. Let's maintain momentum during next week and I once again encourage
you to focus on yourself.

Here is the response to your questions in the agreement.

This is our standard agreement, so we’re not in a position to modify it but
I’ll provided more details below in blue so you have more context in
written on how it will work in practice:

Clause 2.1: Benefits and holiday pay need to be specified, in par...

Hi Ben,

Thanks again for the conversation. I want to briefly recap where we landed
to ensure we’re aligned.

*1. The role (discussion with Adrian on Monday)*
You shared concerns that perception and past track record might work
against you and impact your ability to succeed in the role.

My advice: first align fully with Adrian on expectations, scope, and what
success looks like. If you decide to move forward, we should do so with
clear intent to make it successful - including agreeing upfron...

Hi Johan,

Thanks for the recap, helpful to have things in writing. A couple of points
I want to clarify so we're working from the same understanding.

Hi Ben,

I missed this e-mail. Sorry for the delay.

That is correct. It's a redeployment offer that Adrian wants to align with
you. It's the role at hand or the seperation agreement.

Also wanna remind you that I am confident the formalities in the agreement
will be sorted so appreciate if you can also get back to me on the monetary
aspect. As long as it's still an option.

Thank you

-------------------
Johan Sellgren | HR Business Partner, Experience Mission
| sellgren@spotify.com | Regeri...

Hi Johan,

I've been reflecting on our conversation yesterday. I wanted to capture a
few things in writing so we're aligned on where we are now.

You proposed to withdraw the separation agreement, which was a process
Spotify initiated. I understand that's your current position, and I've
noted it. I'd expect that conversation could be revisited if circumstances
change on either side.

Adrian and I have had productive conversations about scope, and I'm engaged
with that process. What I have at...

Hi Ben,

Following up on our conversations, we can offer you the role of *Staff
Engineer*, reporting to Adrian Ford as outlined and discussed.

You’ll find the main responsibilities outlined in the attached job
description,
<https://docs.google.com/document/d/1evbcJarKpawJXH4qB6JdTaXqv3yP5xCvYLJS305ipe8/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.g54jz8lsravl>
but as you onboard to the role, you and Adrian will need to define
objectives and focus areas together.

The role is full-time and based either at Urban Es...

Hi Johan,

Thanks for your email on 4 March. I've been reviewing it and have a
few questions before I can give you a considered response.

When we last spoke, you mentioned I'd work out the success criteria
with Adrian to avoid the subjectivity of my current situation. We'd
discussed it but I haven't received anything official yet, and with
him out until 16 March I'm not clear on how that gets resolved
beforehand. I'd want a proper understanding of what success looks
like, something concrete...

Hi Jose,

I've attached the termination agreement and my Contract of employment from
Spotify HR.

*Context:* I have been with Spotify for 4 years as a Senior Engineering
Manager within the Content Platform. In this role, I led 6 squads
(approximately 40 engineers) managing critical regulatory and legal
infrastructure. I have consistently been a high performer, with positive
feedback and compensation increases reflecting my standing within the
company’s new performance framework.

Despite the...

Hello!

Apologies for sending this through so late yesterday - I've effectively
stalled for time with Spotify and will be chatting to them again on Monday.

I wanted to clarify something from my latest chat with HR today. This
agreement is NOT due to "a lack of work" and is therefore NOT a redundancy.
Which honestly makes this much more interesting to me.

Cheers,
Ben

Hey,

I am following up on my previous email. I have not heard back yet, but the
situation has evolved and I require your formal assessment before my next
meeting with Spotify HR *next Monday afternoon.*

In a written response today, Spotify HR has confirmed that my proposed
separation is a *"leadership decision"* where they "do not see [me] being
part of this new organization." Crucially, they have pivoted away from the
initial narrative of a "lack of alternative roles" that was mentioned
pr...

Hi Ben!

Thank you for your email.

I’m referring to our conversation yesterday, where we discussed that the employer must negotiate with us if you decline the severance offer and the employer wishes to proceed with a possible termination.
In such negotiations, the employer is also required to consider possible redeployment opportunities within the company.
If we enter into negotiations with the employer and this were to result in a termination, the most we can negotiate for is your right to...

Hi Ben!

Thank you for your email.

I refer to our conversation yesterday as well as my previous email response.

I wish you a pleasant day!

Regards

Helena Borg Jodelsohn 
Labour Law Advisor

Unionen

Contact and opening hours
www.unionen.se/kontakt<http://www.unionen.se/kontakt>

Extraordinary Powers at Work
Do you want to increase your influence/ability to impact your workplace? Here you can read about Become a workplace representative | Unionen<https://www.unionen.se/in-english/become-a-...

Hey Jose,

Thanks for your previous email and the legal assessment of the initial
offer. I wanted to share some important developments from the past week and
get your guidance on how they affect my position.

Quick Context Recap: Spotify offered me a standard Mutual Separation
Agreement (3 months notice + 4 months severance) following a reorganization
of the Content Platform. I've been having ongoing conversations with HR,
initially with Ebba Krusenstierna, now with Johan Sellgren (HRBP for
E...

Hi Jose / Helena,

I hope you're both well. I'm writing with two things: a development on the
agreement clauses, and a follow-up on my earlier email that I haven't heard
back on yet.
1. Employer's Response to Clause Feedback

I forwarded Helena's clause observations to Spotify HR (Johan Sellgren,
HRBP). His response came on the 13th February. Summary below — I'd
appreciate your formal counter-positions on each, particularly the flat
rejections and the competitor clause.

Clause 2.1 (Benefits/...

Hi Benjamin!

Thank you for your email.

To begin with,  the date for when the agreement is to take effect acording to the contract needs to be adjusted, as well as the date from which your notice period begins to run.

The answers you receive from us are recommendations on how we believe the severance agreement should preferably be structured. It is then up to you and the employer to agree on the final design of the agreement. For example, section 1 in your email regarding the pension soluti...

subject: Helena Borg Jodelsohn - Spotify Separation Support
summary: Helena Borg Jodelsohn - Spotify Separation Support

messages.bodyPreview:
Hi Helena,

I hope you're well. I'm Ben Purslow, you provided a clause-by-clause review
of my Spotify separation agreement on 3 February, previously tracked under
the subject "*Jose Manuel - (Benjamin Purslow).*"

I've just sent a follow-up in that thread with Spotify's responses to your
clause feedback and some outstanding legal questions. I wanted to reach out
to you directly as well, as I haven't had a response from Jose since the
case was opened.

Could you let me know whether I should be...

Hi Ben!

Thank you for your email.

You may refer to any one of us. If none of us are available, another advisor will always take over.
I wish you a nice day!

Hälsningar

Helena Borg Jodelsohn 
Facklig Rådgivare

Unionen

Kontaktinformation och öppettider
www.unionen.se/kontakt<http://www.unionen.se/kontakt>

Få krafter utöver det vanliga. På jobbet.

Vill du få ökat inflytande/möjlighet att påverka på din arbetsplats? Här kan du läsa om att bilda klubb: Påverka på jobbet – bli förtroendeval...

subject: Personal and confidential: Amendment to employment
summary: Personal and confidential: Amendment to employment

messages.bodyPreview:
Hi Johan,

Thanks for sending this. I'm forwarding it to my personal email as I've
previously requested, so that I have a permanent record of the document.

I'll review it and come back with any questions.

Thanks,
Ben

Hi Johan,

Thanks for sending through the amendment. I've had a chance to review it
and have two points I need addressed.

   1. I've done the work with Adrian to understand the practical need here
   and what I'd need to do to make this transition work. But the amendment is
   a role change on paper and nothing more. There's nothing in it that
   acknowledges the broader context, nothing that reflects what Spotify is
   committing to in practice, and no indication of what HR's role and
   re...

Thanks for your note  - happy to clarify.

   1. The amendment reflects the formal role change, similar to how your
   employment contract outlines the framework of employment rather than
   operational details. It is not intended to capture broader context or
   internal ways of working. There are no changes to your employment terms
   beyond the role itself, and HR will continue to support the transition in
   line with standard practice.

   2. In relation to the March compensation review,...

Hey Johan,

Thanks for coming back to my questions. You've confirmed the amendment is a
role change on paper with no changes beyond the title, and that the comp
review is closed with my salary unchanged. I note that but it confirms
we're on different pages about what constitutes an offer, and I think
that's why it feels like we're each delayed on the other. For example, I
raised compensation on 9th March; it took until now to get a response, by
which point the review window had closed.

So le...

Hi Ben,

Thanks for outlining your perspective. I understand that you’re looking for
a more holistic view of what this transition entails.

Let me address your points directly:

- Compensation - As mentioned, compensation has been assessed in relation
to the *new *role. This assessment is based on the scope and level of the
Staff Engineer role and aligns with our internal benchmarks. In your case,
the outcome is well positioned within - and above - the expected range for
this level.
When chan...

I realise I mixed up the days and that it’s already Thursday, my apologies.
We can extend the timeline until end of business tomorrow.

If we’re not able to reach an agreement, we would need to explore next
steps, which may include consultation with your union (if applicable). In
that case, I’d appreciate it if you could confirm whether you’re a member
and, if so, which union you belong to.

Thank you

Hi Johan,

I had hoped we'd been working toward a mutually beneficial outcome this
week, and that we were close to aligning on something that reflected both
the contribution I've made to Content Platform and the role you're asking
me to step into. Your response suggests otherwise; there's been no
acknowledgement of what I've delivered, and nothing on the table beyond a
title change.

Given that you've raised the possibility of involving my union, it's clear
we're further apart than I thought....

Hi -

We will be sending a formal consultation request to the union start of next
week to initiate the formal process. All to ensure we move things forward
and eventually find a way foward. Since you are on vacation and an Easter
break for all of us soon it will be time for you to consult with unionen
and the local club. They know the procedure.

Johan

-------------------
Johan Sellgren | HR Business Partner, Experience Mission
| sellgren@spotify.com | Regeringsgatan 19, 111 53 Stockholm |...

subject: Unionen Chef member — formal consultation initiated by Spotify, request for Chefsombudsman
summary: Unionen Chef member — formal consultation initiated by Spotify, request for Chefsombudsman

messages.bodyPreview:
Hi,

I'm writing because my HRBP, Johan Sellgren, informed me in the evening of
Friday 27 March that Spotify will be sending a formal consultation request
to Unionen "start of next week" to initiate what he described as "the
formal process." I want to ensure you have my perspective before that
request arrives.

I'm copying Peter Toth, the local union representative at Spotify, who is
aware of the situation.

I'm a Unionen Chef member (Senior Engineering Manager with personnel
responsibility)....

Hello!
Just confirming that Ben has been in touch with me about his case. I
support his request for help from an ombudsman.

Best regards,
Péter Tóth
chair of the Unionen club at Spotify
